title: [Phase 0] Audit & Organization
description:
Establish clean foundation and organizational structure before adding components.

## Context
Before adding 35+ new components, we must organize the existing 25 components, establish naming conventions, verify all existing components meet quality standards, and create the organizational structure for the expanded library.

## Checklist
- [ ] Audit all 25 existing components (document name, size, layers, purpose)
- [ ] Create section headers matching recommended architecture
- [ ] Establish 8px spacing grid reference
- [ ] Document naming conventions in ADR
- [ ] Verify all existing components have proper vertical text centering
- [ ] Clean up any orphaned layers or symbols
- [ ] Create component inventory spreadsheet/document

## Implementation Notes
**Patterns:**
- Use text layers as section headers (not symbols)
- Headers should be visually distinct (larger font, different color)
- Maintain 48px vertical spacing between sections
- Group related components horizontally within sections

**Anti-patterns:**
- Moving symbols without checking for instance references
- Changing symbol names (breaks existing instances)
- Deleting symbols that may be used elsewhere

**Goals:**
- Clean, navigable component library page
- Clear visual hierarchy between sections
- Easy to find any component

## Rollback Strategy
- Export current state of Components Library page before making changes
- If reorganization causes issues, restore from export
- Keep list of any renamed symbols to revert if needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 25 existing components documented in inventory
- [ ] Components Library page has clear section headers
- [ ] 8px grid visible as reference layer (locked)
- [ ] ADR-0021 created for component naming conventions
- [ ] Zero vertical centering issues in existing components
- [ ] No orphaned or duplicate symbols

## Failure Criteria
- Symbol instances broken after reorganization
- Components missing after cleanup
- Naming inconsistencies introduced
- Section headers misaligned with grid

## Technical Specifications
- Section header text: 16px bold, gray-500
- Section spacing: 48px vertical between sections
- Component spacing: 24px horizontal, 32px vertical within sections
- Grid: 8px base unit

## Dependencies
None - this is the foundation phase

title: [AA.8] Real-time Updates Integration
description:
Wire FileWatcher/WebSocket to automatically refresh issue data. Connect useRealtime hook in app layout, debounce rapid refreshes, show toast on refresh, reconnect on disconnect.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] useRealtime hook connected in root +layout.svelte
- [ ] File changes in .beads/ trigger automatic data refresh within 500ms
- [ ] Rapid file changes debounced — at most one refresh per 500ms window
- [ ] Toast notification shown when data refreshes ('Issues updated')
- [ ] WebSocket disconnection triggers automatic reconnection with backoff
- [ ] Reconnection indicator shown in UI during disconnect
- [ ] No duplicate refreshes when multiple files change simultaneously
- [ ] Works correctly in dev (HMR) and production builds
- [ ] Unit tests for useRealtime hook covering connect/disconnect/refresh/debounce

title: 1.11 File Watching - Real-time updates via chokidar
description:
Chokidar watcher for .beads/*.db and .beads/*.jsonl, WebSocket server for broadcasting changes, client subscription, debounce rapid changes (100ms). File change triggers WebSocket event <1s, client reconnects on disconnect.

## Test Acceptance Criteria

### FileWatcher.test.ts (Server)
- [ ] watches .beads/*.db files
- [ ] watches .beads/*.jsonl files
- [ ] emits change event when file modified
- [ ] debounces rapid changes (100ms)
- [ ] ignores non-database files
- [ ] handles watcher errors gracefully

### WebSocketServer.test.ts
- [ ] accepts client connections
- [ ] broadcasts file:changed events to all clients
- [ ] broadcasts issues:changed events
- [ ] handles client disconnection
- [ ] supports multiple concurrent clients

### useRealtime.test.ts (Client Hook)
- [ ] connects to WebSocket server on mount
- [ ] reconnects on disconnect
- [ ] calls onchange when issues:changed received
- [ ] calls onfilechange when file:changed received
- [ ] disconnects on unmount
- [ ] handles connection errors

### Integration Tests
- [ ] file change propagates to client within 1s
- [ ] client receives correct event payload
- [ ] multiple clients receive same event

## Implementation Notes
- Use TDD: Write tests first (RED), implement (GREEN), refactor
- Record WebSocket vs SSE decision using `adrs new "Real-time Communication Protocol"`
- Use dependency injection for file watcher (ADR-0010)

title: [Phase 1] Create Icon Bridge Layer
description:
Create wrapper symbols with OUR naming convention that contain MD3 icons internally. This maintains backward compatibility - existing components reference 'Icon/Search' and will automatically get the MD3 icon.

## Context
Our components reference icons like 'Icon/Search', 'Icon/Close', etc. Rather than updating every component, we create 'bridge' symbols that:
1. Keep our naming (Icon/Search)
2. Contain the MD3 icon inside (Icons/24/Search)
3. Allow downstream components to auto-update

## Icon Mapping Table

| Our Name | MD3 Source | Notes |
|----------|------------|-------|
| Icon/Search | Icons/24/Search | Search fields |
| Icon/Close | Icons/24/Cross | Close/dismiss |
| Icon/Plus | Icons/24/Add | Add/create |
| Icon/Menu | Icons/24/Menu | Hamburger menu |
| Icon/Check | Icons/24/Check | Checkmarks |
| Icon/Settings | Icons/24/Settings (Outline) | Settings cog |
| Icon/ChevronRight | Icons/24/Chevron - Right | Nav forward |
| Icon/ChevronLeft | Icons/24/Chevron - Left | Nav back |
| Icon/ChevronDown | Icons/24/Arrow - Down | Dropdowns |
| Icon/ChevronUp | Icons/24/Arrow - Up | Collapse |
| Icon/User | Icons/24/Person | User/profile |
| Icon/Calendar | Icons/24/Calendar | Dates |
| Icon/Trash | Icons/24/Delete | Delete action |
| Icon/Edit | Icons/24/Edit | Edit action |
| Icon/Filter | Icons/24/More (vertical) | Filter (closest match) |
| Icon/Home | (custom or MD3 equivalent) | Home nav |
| Icon/Bell | Icons/24/Mail | Notifications |
| Icon/Grid | (keep custom) | Grid view |
| Icon/List | (keep custom) | List view |
| Icon/Refresh | (keep custom or find MD3) | Refresh |
| Icon/Sort | Icons/24/Arrow - Down | Sort indicator |

## Implementation Approach

### Option A: Replace in Place (Recommended)
1. For each existing Icon/* symbol
2. Remove the hand-drawn vector layers
3. Insert MD3 icon instance as child
4. Resize/position to fit 24x24

### Option B: Create New, Swap References
1. Create new Icon-v2/* symbols with MD3
2. Update all component references
3. Delete old symbols

**Recommendation**: Option A is cleaner - same symbol ID, no reference updates needed.

## Checklist
- [ ] Map all 21 current icons to MD3 equivalents
- [ ] Update Icon/Search with MD3 Search
- [ ] Update Icon/Close with MD3 Cross
- [ ] Update Icon/Plus with MD3 Add
- [ ] Update Icon/Menu with MD3 Menu
- [ ] Update Icon/Check with MD3 Check
- [ ] Update Icon/Settings with MD3 Settings
- [ ] Update Icon/ChevronRight with MD3 Chevron - Right
- [ ] Update Icon/ChevronLeft with MD3 Chevron - Left
- [ ] Update Icon/ChevronDown with MD3 Arrow - Down
- [ ] Update Icon/ChevronUp with MD3 Arrow - Up
- [ ] Update Icon/User with MD3 Person
- [ ] Update Icon/Calendar with MD3 Calendar
- [ ] Update Icon/Trash with MD3 Delete
- [ ] Update Icon/Edit with MD3 Edit
- [ ] Keep/improve remaining icons (Filter, Home, Grid, List, Refresh, Sort, Bell)
- [ ] Test all icons render at 24x24
- [ ] Export icon grid comparison (before/after)

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All 21 Icon/* symbols contain MD3 icons (where mapping exists)
2. Icons render crisply at 1x and 2x scale
3. No broken symbol references in downstream components
4. Icon sizing consistent (24x24)
5. Visual quality noticeably improved from hand-drawn versions

## Testing
After each icon update:
1. Check symbol renders in Components Library
2. Check a component using that icon (e.g., Button/Icon uses Icon/Settings)
3. Verify no visual regression

## Dependencies
- Phase 0 must be complete (MD3 icons imported)

title: MD3 Components Library Integration
description:
Rebuild the Components Library using Material Design 3 as a foundation for higher quality icons and components. This replaces the hand-drawn vector icons and fixes text alignment issues through a systematic, dependency-aware approach.

## Problem Statement
Current Components Library has quality issues:
- Hand-drawn vector icons display poorly
- Text alignment inconsistent across components
- Visual quality below professional standards

## Solution
Import Material Design 3 primitives and build a bridge layer that:
1. Maps our naming conventions to MD3 symbols
2. Maintains backward compatibility with existing wireframes
3. Progressively improves each layer of the component hierarchy

## Component Dependency Chain
```
MD3 Foundation (External Library)
        ↓
   Icon Bridge (Our naming → MD3 icons)
        ↓
   Atomic Layer (Buttons, Badges, Avatars)
        ↓
   Form Layer (Inputs, Dropdowns, TextAreas)
        ↓
   Composite Layer (Cards, Activity, Legends)
        ↓
   Layout Layer (Nav, Modal, Tabs, Search)
        ↓
   Domain Layer (Gantt, Kanban, Records)
```

## Success Criteria
1. All icons use MD3 primitives (no hand-drawn vectors)
2. Text alignment consistent across all components
3. Components render crisply at 1x and 2x
4. No broken symbol references
5. Wireframes continue to work with updated components

title: [Phase 1] Create Icon Component Library
description:
# Phase 1: Icon Component Library

Create reusable icon symbols that will be used across buttons, inputs, navigation, and other components.

Icons are used by many other components (buttons, inputs, cards, nav). They must exist before we can properly rebuild those components with icon support.

- Icon/*/20: 20x20px (buttons, standalone)

title: [Phase 0] Establish Design Token Foundation
description:
# Phase 0: Design Token Foundation

Create a design token reference system that all components will use for consistent styling.

## Why This Comes First
Design tokens are the foundation for ALL components. Colors, typography, spacing, and radii must be defined before building any component to ensure consistency.

## Deliverables

### 1. Color Palette Token Frame
Create a reference frame in Sketch showing all colors:

```
Neutrals (Gray scale):
- neutral-50:  #F9FAFB  (backgrounds)
- neutral-100: #F3F4F6  (hover states)
- neutral-200: #E5E7EB  (borders)
- neutral-300: #D1D5DB  (disabled)
- neutral-400: #9CA3AF  (placeholder text)
- neutral-500: #6B7280  (secondary text)
- neutral-600: #4B5563  (body text)
- neutral-700: #374151  (headings)
- neutral-800: #1F2937  (nav background)
- neutral-900: #111827  (primary text)

Primary (Blue):

- primary-600: #2563EB  (hover)
```

### 4. Border Radius Token Frame

title: Components Library Rebuild
description:
# Epic: Components Library Rebuild

Comprehensive rebuild of the Sketch Components Library to fix structural issues and add missing components.

## Problem Statement
When frames were converted to symbols, internal layers (text, icons, nested elements) were lost. Components are now unusable - they're just colored rectangles without labels or interactive elements.

## Goals
1. Rebuild all 58 components with proper internal structure
2. Add missing foundational elements (icons, design tokens)
3. Add missing component categories (alerts, tables)
4. Establish consistent naming and sizing conventions
5. Enable proper symbol overrides for customization

## Success Metrics
- 100% of components have proper internal structure
- 100% of text elements are overridable
- 0 duplicate or empty symbols
- >80% of wireframe elements use symbols
- No visual regression in wireframes

## Phases
- Phase 0: Foundation (Design Tokens)
- Phase 1: Icons
- Phase 2: Atomic Components
- Phase 3: Form Elements
- Phase 4: Composite Components
- Phase 5: Layout Components
- Phase 6: Domain-Specific Components

## Technical Context
- Tool: Sketch with MCP API
- Wireframes: 13 views in looms-wireframes.sketch
- Current symbols: 58 (all broken)
- Export location: .claude/plans/unified-beads-webui/wireframes/exports/

title: Charts Library: Horizontal Bar Chart (Aging WIP) Symbol
description:
Create reusable Horizontal Bar Chart Sketch symbol at Components/Chart/HorizontalBarChart for Aging WIP visualization.

**What**: Horizontal bar chart showing in-progress issue ages. Each bar represents an issue, length = age in days. Color-coded by health (green/amber/red based on percentile thresholds). Used to identify stale work items.
**Visual spec**: Frame 400x240. Horizontal bars sorted by age (longest at top), Y-axis shows issue IDs/titles, X-axis shows days. Vertical percentile lines at 85th and 95th. Bar color: green (<85th), amber (85th-95th), red (>95th).
**Overrides**: Bar colors (3 tiers), percentile line positions, axis labels.
**Used in**: Dashboard wireframe (Aging WIP section), WIP detail view.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Data I/O, Flow, and Schema
- [ ] Define input schema: `{ items: { id: string, label: string, value: number, category?: string }[] }`
- [ ] Sort by value (highest/oldest first)
- [ ] Support category grouping
- [ ] Accept data via: hardcoded, JSON file, or script injection

### Math: Values → Coordinates
- [ ] Scale bar widths to max value
- [ ] Calculate row heights based on item count
- [ ] Support threshold markers (e.g., SLA boundaries)
- [ ] Color mapping based on value ranges (RAG status)

### Rect Generation for Bars
- [ ] Generate horizontal rectangles
- [ ] Bar width proportional to value
- [ ] Consistent bar height with gaps
- [ ] Color based on aging threshold (green < 7d, yellow < 14d, red >= 14d)

### Inventory of Included Shapes
- [ ] Container frame (background)
- [ ] Item labels (left side)
- [ ] Horizontal bars
- [ ] Value labels (end of bar or inside)
- [ ] Threshold lines (vertical)
- [ ] Legend for thresholds
- [ ] Category headers (if grouped)

### Variablization API/Table for Symbols
| Override | Type | Default | Description |
|----------|------|---------|-------------|
| itemCount | number | 10 | Number of items to show |
| showThresholds | boolean | true | Show aging thresholds |
| sortOrder | enum | desc | asc/desc by value |
| groupByCategory | boolean | false | Group by status/category |

### Variablization API/Table for Components
| Component | Overridable Properties |
|-----------|----------------------|
| Bar | fill color, height |
| ItemLabel | text, color, font size |
| ValueLabel | text, color, position |
| ThresholdLine | x position, color, label |

### Typography Variablization
| Element | Token | Default |
|---------|-------|---------|
| Item labels | `chart.item.label` | Inter 13px Medium Gray-900 |
| Value labels | `chart.value.label` | Inter 11px Gray-600 |
| Threshold labels | `chart.threshold.label` | Inter 10px Gray-500 |
| Category headers | `chart.category.header` | Inter 12px Bold Gray-700 |

### Color/Palette Variablization
- [ ] RAG colors: `{ green: <7d, yellow: 7-14d, red: >14d }`
- [ ] Map to design tokens: `status.success`, `status.warning`, `status.error`
- [ ] Threshold line colors
- [ ] Dark mode variants

### Plugin Logic for Data Source Updates
- [ ] Script to calculate age from issue dates
- [ ] Sort and filter logic
- [ ] Regenerate on data change
- [ ] Configurable thresholds

### Vector Shape Generation
- [ ] Bars as horizontal Rectangle shapes
- [ ] Threshold lines as vertical Path shapes
- [ ] Exportable as SVG
- [ ] Layer naming: `Chart/AgingWIP/{item}`

title: Charts Library: Area Chart / CFD Symbol
description:
Create reusable Stacked Area Chart Sketch symbol at Components/Chart/AreaChart for CFD (Cumulative Flow Diagram) visualization.

**What**: Stacked area chart showing cumulative issue counts by status over time. The signature lean/agile metrics chart — each colored band represents a status, and band width indicates WIP at that time. Used to spot bottlenecks (widening bands) and flow problems.
**Visual spec**: Frame 400x240. Five stacked areas: Open (bottom), In Progress, Review, Done, Closed (top). X-axis = dates, Y-axis = cumulative count. Hover tooltip area. Date range selector above chart.
**Overrides**: Area colors (5 status colors), axis labels, date range labels, tooltip placeholder.
**Used in**: Dashboard wireframe (CFD section), metrics detail view, team health reports.
**Design reference**: Based on lean/kanban CFD conventions.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Data I/O, Flow, and Schema
- [ ] Define input schema: `{ dates: string[], series: { name: string, values: number[], color: string }[] }`
- [ ] Series must stack (each value adds to previous)
- [ ] Support cumulative and non-cumulative modes
- [ ] Accept data via: hardcoded, JSON file, or script injection
- [ ] Output: Vector shape group with named layers

### Math: Values → Coordinates
- [ ] Calculate stacked Y positions (cumulative sum)
- [ ] Map dates to X axis (time-based spacing)
- [ ] Scale total height to chart bounds
- [ ] Handle sparse data (missing dates)

### Path Generation for Areas
- [ ] Generate closed Path shapes for each stacked area
- [ ] Bottom edge follows previous series' top edge
- [ ] Top edge connects data points
- [ ] Support smooth curve interpolation
- [ ] Areas must not overlap (proper stacking)

### Inventory of Included Shapes
- [ ] Container frame (background)
- [ ] X-axis with date labels
- [ ] Y-axis with count labels
- [ ] Stacked area shapes (one per status)
- [ ] Grid lines (horizontal)
- [ ] Legend showing status colors
- [ ] Optional: trend lines, markers

### Variablization API/Table for Symbols
| Override | Type | Default | Description |
|----------|------|---------|-------------|
| seriesCount | number | 4 | Status categories (open, in_progress, review, done) |
| dateRange | number | 30 | Days to display |
| showLegend | boolean | true | Show legend |
| showGrid | boolean | true | Show grid lines |
| curveType | enum | monotone | linear/monotone |

### Variablization API/Table for Components
| Component | Overridable Properties |
|-----------|----------------------|
| Area | fill color, opacity |
| AxisLabel | text, color, rotation |
| Legend | position, orientation |
| Grid | stroke color, opacity |

### Typography Variablization
| Element | Token | Default |
|---------|-------|---------|
| Date labels | `chart.axis.label` | Inter 10px Gray-500 (rotated -45°) |
| Count labels | `chart.value.label` | Inter 10px Gray-600 |
| Legend text | `chart.legend.text` | Inter 11px Gray-700 |
| Status names | `chart.series.name` | Inter 11px Medium Gray-800 |
| Title | `chart.title` | Inter 14px Bold Gray-900 |

### Color/Palette Variablization
- [ ] Status-based colors: `{ open: blue, in_progress: yellow, review: purple, done: green }`
- [ ] Map to design tokens: `status.open`, `status.inProgress`, etc.
- [ ] Support custom color schemes
- [ ] Dark mode variants with adjusted opacity

### Plugin Logic for Data Source Updates
- [ ] Script to generate CFD from issue history
- [ ] Script to regenerate areas from flow data
- [ ] Calculate cumulative values from daily snapshots
- [ ] Support date range filtering

### Vector Shape Generation
- [ ] Areas as closed Path shapes with fills
- [ ] Proper z-order (bottom series rendered first)
- [ ] Exportable as SVG
- [ ] Layer naming: `Chart/CFD/{status}/Area`

title: 2.9 Date Prefix Parsing - Timeline placement
description:
Parse date prefixes from issue titles for timeline placement. Support formats: [Q1], [Q2 2025], [H1], [W12], [Jan], [2025-03-15]. Return type, year, period, startDate, endDate. Handle year rollover, null for invalid.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] parseDatePrefix(title) extracts and parses prefix from title string
- [ ] Supported formats: [Q1], [Q2 2025], [H1], [H2 2025], [W12], [Jan], [Mar 2025], [2025-03-15]
- [ ] Returns { type, year, period, startDate, endDate } or null for no match
- [ ] Year defaults to current year when omitted
- [ ] Quarter: Q1=Jan-Mar, Q2=Apr-Jun, Q3=Jul-Sep, Q4=Oct-Dec
- [ ] Half: H1=Jan-Jun, H2=Jul-Dec
- [ ] Week: ISO week number to date range
- [ ] Handles year rollover (e.g., [Q1] in December means next year)
- [ ] Unit tests for each format including edge cases

title: 2.1 Metrics Engine - Lean/Kanban calculations
description:
Core calculation engine for lean metrics. Implement calculateLeadTime, calculateCycleTime, calculateThroughput, calculateCFD, calculateAgingWIP, calculatePercentiles. Calculations match beads-dashboard, exclude wisps, <500ms for 1000 issues.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Module exports: calculateLeadTime, calculateCycleTime, calculateThroughput, calculateCFD, calculateAgingWIP, calculatePercentiles
- [ ] Lead time = closed_at - created_at (days); cycle time = closed_at - started_at (days)
- [ ] Throughput counts closed issues per time bucket (day/week/month)
- [ ] CFD returns cumulative counts per status per date
- [ ] Aging WIP returns open issues with age = now - created_at
- [ ] Percentiles return P50, P85, P95 from sorted durations
- [ ] All calculations exclude wisps (ephemeral issues)
- [ ] Performance: <500ms for 1000 issues dataset
- [ ] Unit tests with fixture data for each calculation

title: Database Switcher UI
description:
Add a database switcher that lets users swap between Dolt databases on the same server without changing connection settings.

**What**: The shared Dolt server on localhost:13306 hosts multiple project databases (beads_looms, beads_forge, beads_research, etc.). Users should be able to switch between them from the UI — e.g., from looms to forge to research — without editing config files or restarting the app.

**Components**:
- Database selector dropdown in the app header (or within the settings modal from looms-1lp)
- Dropdown lists all available databases on the connected server (via SHOW DATABASES, filtered to beads_* prefix)
- Current database highlighted/selected
- Switching triggers DAL reconnect to the new database
- Optional: show issue count per database in the dropdown for context

**Behavior**:
- On open: queries SHOW DATABASES from the connected Dolt server
- Filters to beads_* databases (excludes information_schema, mysql, dolt system DBs)
- On select: reconnects DAL pool to the new database name
- Page data reloads automatically after switch
- Current selection persisted to local storage (survives page refresh)
- Does NOT modify .beads/config.yaml (this is a session-level switch, not a config change)

**Relationship to looms-1lp**: This feature extends the Database Configuration UI. looms-1lp handles connection settings (host/port); this handles which database on that server.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Database selector visible in app header or settings panel
- [ ] Dropdown lists all beads_* databases from the connected server
- [ ] Current database visually indicated in the selector
- [ ] Selecting a different database reconnects the DAL without app restart
- [ ] Page data reloads with issues from the newly selected database
- [ ] Selection persisted in local storage across page refreshes
- [ ] System databases (information_schema, mysql, dolt) filtered from list
- [ ] Error shown if selected database has no issues table
close_reason: DatabaseSwitcher dropdown in GlobalNav header, lists beads_* databases, switches via PUT /api/config/database, persists selection in localStorage, triggers data reload

title: Database Configuration UI
description:
Add a database configuration interface accessible from the app settings.

**What**: Users need to view and modify the Dolt server connection settings (host, port, database name) from within the Looms WebUI instead of manually editing config files. This enables easier setup and troubleshooting when the database connection changes.

**Components**:
- Settings/config icon in the app header (gear icon)
- "Database" button/tab within settings
- Modal dialog showing current connection config:
  - Backend type (Dolt/SQLite)
  - Host (e.g., 127.0.0.1)
  - Port (e.g., 13306)
  - Database name (e.g., beads_looms)
  - Connection status indicator (connected/disconnected)
- Save/Apply button that validates the connection before persisting
- Cancel button to discard changes

**Behavior**:
- On open: reads current config from server and displays it
- On save: validates connection (test query), then persists to .beads/config.yaml
- Shows error if connection test fails (with actionable message)
- Does NOT require app restart — reconnects the DAL with new config

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gear icon visible in app header, opens settings panel
- [ ] Database tab/button within settings navigates to DB config
- [ ] Modal displays current backend, host, port, database name, and connection status
- [ ] Save button validates connection before persisting changes
- [ ] Error message shown if connection test fails
- [ ] Config changes persisted to .beads/config.yaml
- [ ] DAL reconnects with new config without requiring app restart
- [ ] Cancel button closes modal without saving
close_reason: Gear icon in GlobalNav, DatabaseConfigModal with connection test/save/apply, API endpoints GET/PUT/POST at /api/config/database, DAL reconnect without restart

title: [Phase 3.6] Create InlineEdit/View component
description:
Create the InlineEdit/View Sketch symbol at Components/InlineEdit/View.

**What**: Display mode for inline-editable text fields. Shows the current value as static text with an edit affordance (pencil icon) that appears on hover to signal editability. Clicking transitions to InlineEdit/Edit state.
**Visual spec**: Size 164x24. Text value (body-medium, on-surface) left-aligned, trailing edit icon (16x16, on-surface-variant, pencil/edit_note). Icon hidden by default, visible on hover. Subtle underline-on-hover (1px, primary token) below text to reinforce editability. 4px horizontal padding, 4px vertical padding.
**Overrides**: Display text, text color, edit icon (symbol override), hover underline visibility (for read-only variant), icon visibility toggle.
**Used in**: Issue detail title, description preview, editable metadata fields (status, priority labels).
**Design reference**: MD3 inline editing pattern, Yoga editable text.

- [ ] Component symbol created in Sketch under Components/ hierarchy
- [ ] Symbol aligned to 8px grid
- [ ] Overrides configured for customizable properties (text, color, icon)
- [ ] Symbol renders correctly in isolation and when inserted into wireframes

title: [Phase 3.5] Create Avatar/Stack component
description:
Create the Avatar/Stack Sketch symbol at Components/Avatar/Stack.

**What**: Overlapping circular avatars displaying multiple assignees or team members in a compact horizontal strip. Shows up to 3 visible avatars with a "+N" overflow indicator when more exist.
**Visual spec**: Size 92x32. Three overlapping circles (32px diameter each, -8px horizontal overlap between adjacent). Each circle has 2px white border (surface token) to separate from neighbors. Overflow indicator is a 32px circle with surface-container-highest background and label-small "+N" text. Avatars stack right-to-left (first avatar on top).
**Overrides**: Avatar 1/2/3 image or initials text, avatar 1/2/3 background color (for initials mode), overflow count text, overflow circle visibility (hide when 3 or fewer), avatar 2/3 visibility (for 1- or 2-person stacks).

title: [Phase 1.2] Create Toast/Success component

**Visual spec**: Size 280x48. Green #22c55e left accent bar (4px wide) or background tint. White or light background fill. Left-aligned check-circle icon (16x16, green). Body text in dark gray (#1f2937), 14px. Optional dismiss X button (16x16) right-aligned. 8px internal padding, 4px border-radius.
**Overrides**: Message text (string), icon symbol (swap), dismiss button visibility (boolean), background opacity.
**Used in**: All views — issue list (after bulk actions), issue detail (after save/update), settings (after config change), kanban board (after card move).
**Design reference**: MD3 Snackbar with custom color mapping; Yoga notification pattern.

title: Component Library Expansion - 35+ New Components
description:
Expand the Looms wireframe component library from 25 to 60+ components following industry best practices from Yoga, Primer, Elastic EUI, ANT Design, and Lexicon design systems.

## Scope
- Phase 0: Audit & Organization
- Phase 1: Foundations & Feedback (6 components)
- Phase 2: Enhanced Data Entry (8 components)
- Phase 3: Data Display (6 components)
- Phase 4: Overlay System (5 components)
- Phase 5: Complex Composites (5 components)
- Phase 6: Domain Enhancements (5 components)

## Guiding Principles
1. Progressive Enhancement - Build foundations before complex components
2. Dependency Awareness - Components that depend on others come after their dependencies
3. Test-Driven - Each component has acceptance criteria verified before proceeding
4. Clean As We Go - Organize and document while building
5. Compound Patterns - Use Component.Subcomponent architecture where appropriate

See: .claude/plans/component-library-expansion/index.md

title: Charts Library: Throughput Chart Symbol
description:
Create a reusable throughput chart symbol for items-completed-per-period visualization.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Data I/O, Flow, and Schema
- [ ] Define input schema: `{ periods: { label: string, value: number }[], target?: number }`
- [ ] Support 4-12 periods (weeks/sprints)
- [ ] Auto-scale Y-axis with padding

### Math: Value → Coordinates
- [ ] `barX = chartLeft + (index * (barWidth + gap))`
- [ ] `barHeight = (value / maxValue) * chartHeight`
- [ ] `targetY = chartBottom - (target / maxValue) * chartHeight`

### Rect Generation
- [ ] Fixed bar width, configurable gap
- [ ] Top-rounded corners (4px)
- [ ] Target line as dashed horizontal

### Inventory of Shapes
- [ ] ChartBg (Rectangle) - chart area
- [ ] Grid/H{n} (Line) - horizontal grid
- [ ] Bar/{period} (Rectangle) - throughput bars
- [ ] {value} (Text) - value labels
- [ ] {period} (Text) - period labels
- [ ] TargetLine (Line) - target threshold
- [ ] Target: {value} (Text) - target label

### Variablization API
- [ ] Bar color overridable
- [ ] Target line visibility/value
- [ ] Period label format

### Color/Palette Tokens
- [ ] Bars: blue-500
- [ ] Above target: green-500
- [ ] Below target: amber-500
- [ ] Target line: gray-400 dashed

### Typography
- [ ] Title: 14px semibold
- [ ] Value labels: 10px semibold
- [ ] Period labels: 10px regular
- [ ] Target label: 10px medium gray-500

- [ ] `binX = chartLeft + (index * binWidth)`

title: Set up SQLite FTS5 for full-text search
description:
Configure SQLite Full-Text Search (FTS5) for efficient text searching in title and description fields.

## Requirements
- Create FTS5 virtual table for issues
- Index title and description fields
- Set up triggers to keep FTS index in sync with issues table
- Configure tokenizer for code-friendly search (preserve underscores, camelCase)

## Schema
```sql
-- FTS5 virtual table
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE issues_fts USING fts5(
  id,
  title,
  description,
  content='issues',
  content_rowid='rowid',
  tokenize='porter unicode61'
);

-- Triggers to keep in sync
CREATE TRIGGER issues_ai AFTER INSERT ON issues BEGIN
  INSERT INTO issues_fts(rowid, id, title, description)
  VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.description);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER issues_ad AFTER DELETE ON issues BEGIN
  INSERT INTO issues_fts(issues_fts, rowid, id, title, description)
  VALUES('delete', old.rowid, old.id, old.title, old.description);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER issues_au AFTER UPDATE ON issues BEGIN
  INSERT INTO issues_fts(issues_fts, rowid, id, title, description)
  VALUES('delete', old.rowid, old.id, old.title, old.description);
  INSERT INTO issues_fts(rowid, id, title, description)
  VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.description);
END;
```

## Location
`src/lib/server/db/migrations/XXX_add_fts5.sql`

Parent: looms-i9d

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FTS5 virtual table created indexing issue title and description
- [ ] Insert/update/delete triggers keep FTS index in sync with issues table
- [ ] Tokenizer configured for code-friendly search (preserves underscores, camelCase)
- [ ] Migration file at src/lib/server/db/migrations/
- [ ] Full-text queries return relevant results with ranking
- [ ] No SQL injection possible through search input

title: Implement server-side search executor (SQL)
Implement client-side search executor
Implement search query parser
description:
Create a parser that converts search query strings into an AST (Abstract Syntax Tree) that can be executed by both client and server executors.

## Input
```
status:open priority:>=2 -wontfix && (type:bug || type:task)
```

## Output AST
```typescript
interface SearchQuery {
  terms: SearchTerm[];
  operator: 'AND' | 'OR';
}

interface SearchTerm {
  field: string;
  operator: 'eq' | 'ne' | 'gt' | 'gte' | 'lt' | 'lte' | 'contains' | 'regex' | 'exists';
  value: string | number | Date | RegExp;
  negated: boolean;
}
```

## Requirements
- Handle all operator syntax from ADR-0023
- Produce identical AST regardless of execution target
- Include validation and helpful error messages
- Support parentheses for grouping
- Handle quoted strings with escapes

## Location
`src/lib/search/parser.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parser converts query strings to SearchQuery AST
- [ ] All operators from ADR-0023 supported: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, contains, regex, exists
- [ ] Parentheses grouping for AND/OR logic works correctly
- [ ] Negation prefix (-) handled
- [ ] Quoted strings with escape characters parsed correctly
- [ ] Validation returns helpful error messages for malformed queries
- [ ] Parser produces identical AST regardless of execution target
- [ ] Module located at src/lib/search/parser.ts

title: Advanced search operators for issue filtering
description:
Implement a comprehensive search query system with operators for filtering issues and epics. Supports field matching, negation, boolean logic, comparisons, regex, date ranges, and more.

## Operator Syntax

| Category | Syntax | Example |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Field match | field:value | status:open |
| Negation | -term or !term | -wontfix |
| Boolean | &&, \|\| | bug && urgent |
| Comparison | >, >=, <, <= | priority:>=2 |
| Contains | field:"phrase" | title:"login form" |
| Regex | field:re:/pattern/ | id:re:/PROJ-\d+/ |
| Date relative | field:<Nd | created:<7d |
| Range | field:min..max | priority:1..3 |
| Exists | has:field | has:assignee |
| Multiple | field:a\|b\|c | type:bug\|task |

## Architecture

Uses hybrid client-side + server-side approach per ADR-0023:
- Client-side for instant filtering of cached data
- Server-side with FTS5 for full-text and large datasets
- Same query parser serves both executors

title: WebSocket Subscription Model for Gas-Town
description:
Per-channel WebSocket subscriptions for Gas-Town events. Subscribe to gastown:status, gastown:rigs, gastown:rig:{name}, gastown:polecats, gastown:convoys, gastown:mail. Server-side routing with wildcard matching.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WebSocket client supports per-channel subscriptions (subscribe/unsubscribe)
- [ ] Channels implemented: gastown:status, gastown:rigs, gastown:rig:{name}, gastown:polecats, gastown:convoys, gastown:mail
- [ ] Server-side routing dispatches events to subscribed channels only
- [ ] Wildcard matching supported (e.g., gastown:rig:* receives all rig events)
- [ ] Connection auto-reconnects on drop with exponential backoff
- [ ] Subscription state restored after reconnection

title: 5.11 Project Tags
description:
Tag management UI for tag-based project organization. Create/edit/delete tags, filter by tags, tag colors, bulk tag operations. Tags visible on cards.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tag management UI supports create, edit, and delete operations
- [ ] Tags have customizable colors (preset palette)
- [ ] Issue cards display assigned tags as colored badges
- [ ] Filter-by-tag narrows issue list to matching items
- [ ] Bulk tag operations: add/remove tag across selected issues
- [ ] Tag names validated for uniqueness and length

title: 5.8 SQL Explorer
description:
SQLExplorer.svelte for advanced SQL queries. Query editor with syntax highlighting, results table with sorting, query history, save favorites, export CSV/JSON. Execute SELECT queries <500ms.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SQLExplorer.svelte provides query editor with SQL syntax highlighting
- [ ] Results displayed in sortable table with column headers
- [ ] SELECT queries execute in <500ms for typical issue datasets
- [ ] Query history persists across sessions (local storage)
- [ ] Favorite queries can be saved and recalled by name
- [ ] Export results to CSV or JSON format
- [ ] Non-SELECT statements rejected with clear error message

title: 5.7 Knowledge Panel (Memory)
description:
KnowledgePanel.svelte for browsing agent memory and knowledge base. Memory file browser (.beads/memory/), content viewer (markdown), search within memory, link memory to issues.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] KnowledgePanel.svelte displays file browser for .beads/memory/ directory
- [ ] Content viewer renders markdown files with syntax highlighting
- [ ] Search within memory files returns matching results with context
- [ ] Memory entries can be linked to specific issues
- [ ] File tree supports expand/collapse for nested directories
- [ ] Read-only: no editing of memory files from the UI

title: 5.5 Mail System Integration
description:
MailInbox.svelte, MailMessage.svelte, ComposeMail.svelte for agent-to-human communication. Unread badge in header, mark as read, reply functionality. Real-time updates via WebSocket.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MailInbox.svelte lists messages with sender, subject, and timestamp
- [ ] Unread badge displays in app header with unread count
- [ ] Clicking a message opens MailMessage view and marks as read
- [ ] ComposeMail.svelte allows replying to agent messages
- [ ] Real-time updates via WebSocket push new messages without refresh
- [ ] Empty state shown when inbox is empty

title: 5.4 Convoy Tracking
description:
ConvoyList.svelte, ConvoyCard.svelte, ConvoyDetail.svelte components. Progress visualization, status filtering, create convoy modal. Lists convoys, shows progress, links to constituent issues.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ConvoyList.svelte lists all convoys with status and progress
- [ ] ConvoyCard shows convoy name, status, and completion percentage
- [ ] ConvoyDetail expands to show constituent issues with status
- [ ] Filter convoys by status (active/completed/failed)
- [ ] Create convoy modal with issue selector and name field
- [ ] Progress visualization updates as child issues close

title: 5.3 Agent Monitoring Dashboard
description:
AgentDashboard.svelte, RigCard.svelte, PolecatCard.svelte components. Status indicators (working/stalled/zombie), quick actions (start/stop/nuke), auto-refresh (30s). Shows all rigs and polecats, current issue per polecat.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AgentDashboard.svelte displays all rigs and their polecats
- [ ] RigCard shows rig name, status, and polecat count
- [ ] PolecatCard shows polecat name, status (working/stalled/zombie), and current issue
- [ ] Quick actions: start, stop, nuke available per rig/polecat
- [ ] Status auto-refreshes every 30 seconds
- [ ] Stalled/zombie polecats visually distinguished (color/icon)

title: 5.2 gt CLI Integration
description:
GasTownCLI wrapper class mapping all gt commands: status, doctor, rig list/status/start/stop, polecat list/status, sling, convoy list/status/create, mail inbox/send/read. JSON output parsing, error handling, timeout handling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GasTownCLI wrapper exposes methods for: status, doctor, rig list/status/start/stop, polecat list/status, sling, convoy list/status/create, mail inbox/send/read
- [ ] All commands parse JSON output from gt CLI
- [ ] Timeout handling prevents hung commands (configurable, default 30s)
- [ ] Error responses include gt stderr message for debugging
- [ ] Wrapper is injectable/mockable for testing

title: 5.1 Gas-Town Detection
description:
Detect Gas-Town installation via gt --version, town status via gt status --json --fast. Config parsing, feature flag integration, graceful degradation when not installed. Shows Gas-Town features only when available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `gt --version` checked on startup to detect Gas-Town installation
- [ ] `gt status --json --fast` used to determine town status
- [ ] Gas-Town UI features hidden when gt CLI not installed
- [ ] Graceful degradation: no errors or broken UI when Gas-Town absent
- [ ] Feature flags toggle Gas-Town sections independently
- [ ] Config parsing reads Gas-Town project settings when available

title: WebSocket Server Architecture
description:
Implement WebSocket server via SvelteKit hooks + ws library. Connection management with client tracking, broadcast functionality, session-specific events, client reconnection with exponential backoff.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WebSocket server implemented via SvelteKit hooks + ws library
- [ ] Connection management tracks connected clients with unique IDs
- [ ] Broadcast functionality sends messages to all connected clients
- [ ] Session-specific events routed only to the relevant client
- [ ] Client reconnection with exponential backoff (max 30s)
- [ ] Server handles graceful shutdown (close all connections)
- [ ] Health endpoint reports active connection count

title: 4.11 Interaction History
description:
Interaction log viewer showing human-agent interactions. Filter by session/issue, approval/rejection history, analytics on approval rate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Interaction log viewer displays human-agent interactions chronologically
- [ ] Filter by session or linked issue
- [ ] Approval/rejection history shown with reason and timestamp
- [ ] Analytics summary: approval rate, average review time
- [ ] Click on interaction navigates to the originating session

title: 4.10 Agent Config UI
description:
AgentConfig.svelte for configuring agent behavior. Default prompts per issue type, auto-approve rules, timeout settings, worktree preferences. Settings persist in local storage, per-project configuration.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AgentConfig.svelte renders form for agent behavior settings
- [ ] Default prompts configurable per issue type (task, bug, feature)
- [ ] Auto-approve rules editable (list of allowed operations)
- [ ] Timeout settings adjustable per agent operation
- [ ] Worktree preference toggle (use worktrees vs. main branch)
- [ ] Settings persist in local storage
- [ ] Per-project configuration: settings scoped to current project
- [ ] Reset-to-defaults button available

title: 4.9 Session Persistence/Resumption
description:
Session state serialization, resume button for paused sessions, context restoration, output history retrieval. Sessions survive page refresh, can resume paused sessions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session state serialized to persistent storage on pause/exit
- [ ] Resume button available for paused sessions in the history view
- [ ] Context restoration loads prior session state (prompt, output, plan)
- [ ] Output history retrievable for past sessions
- [ ] Sessions survive page refresh without data loss
- [ ] Resuming a session restores terminal output and scroll position

title: 4.8 Claude Planning Integration
description:
Plan viewer component showing Claude's plan steps, step status tracking, manual step override, plan modification. Steps update as completed, can skip/modify steps, saves modifications.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Plan viewer component renders Claude's plan steps as a checklist
- [ ] Step status updates in real-time as agent completes steps
- [ ] Manual step override allows marking steps complete/skipped
- [ ] Plan modifications saved and reflected in the viewer
- [ ] Steps can be reordered or removed from the plan
- [ ] Plan state persists across page reloads

title: 4.7 ReTake (Retry) Workflow
description:
ReTake button on rejected items, modified prompt input, preserve context option, new session creation linking old and new sessions. Retry count tracked.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ReTake button available on rejected verification items
- [ ] Modified prompt input allows adjusting instructions before retry
- [ ] Preserve-context option retains prior session context in new session
- [ ] New session linked to original session for traceability
- [ ] Retry count tracked and displayed on the item
- [ ] ReTake launches a new agent session with updated prompt

title: 4.6 Approve/Reject Workflow
description:
Approve button with confirmation, reject button with reason input, diff viewer for code changes, commit message preview, quick approve keyboard shortcut. Approve commits/pushes changes, reject reverts changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Approve button with confirmation dialog commits and pushes changes
- [ ] Reject button requires reason input before proceeding
- [ ] Diff viewer shows code changes for review before approve/reject
- [ ] Commit message preview shown before approval
- [ ] Quick-approve keyboard shortcut available (e.g., Ctrl+Enter)
- [ ] Rejection reverts agent changes in the worktree
- [ ] Audit trail: approval/rejection logged with actor and timestamp

title: 4.5 Verification Queue
description:
VerificationQueue.svelte and VerificationItem.svelte for agent outputs awaiting human verification. Queue badge showing pending count, filter by type/status, sort by age. Items link to session.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VerificationQueue.svelte displays list of agent outputs awaiting review
- [ ] Queue badge in app header shows pending verification count
- [ ] Items filterable by type (code change, config, etc.) and status
- [ ] Items sortable by age (oldest first by default)
- [ ] Each VerificationItem links to the originating session
- [ ] Empty state when no items pending verification

title: 4.4 Agent History View
description:
AgentHistory.svelte showing past agent sessions. Session list with filters, view past session output, session metadata (duration, outcome), link to issue.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AgentHistory.svelte lists past agent sessions in reverse chronological order
- [ ] Session list filterable by date range, outcome, and linked issue
- [ ] Clicking a session shows its full output log
- [ ] Session metadata displayed: duration, outcome (success/fail/aborted), linked issue
- [ ] Link from session to its associated issue navigates to issue detail
- [ ] Empty state when no past sessions exist

title: 4.3 Session Output Streaming
description:
PTY integration for live output, ANSI color support, output buffering, scroll-to-bottom behavior, pause/resume scrolling. Output appears <100ms after generation, colors render correctly, large outputs don't freeze UI.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PTY integration streams agent output to terminal in real-time
- [ ] ANSI color codes rendered correctly in output display
- [ ] Output appears within 100ms of generation (latency target)
- [ ] Scroll-to-bottom behavior follows new output automatically
- [ ] Pause/resume scrolling toggle available (click or keyboard)
- [ ] Large outputs (>10k lines) do not freeze the UI (buffered rendering)

title: 4.2 Agent Session Launch
description:
LaunchAgentModal.svelte and session manager service. Launch via claude CLI, worktree selection, custom prompt input, session state tracking. Single focused session model - only one active session at a time.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] LaunchAgentModal.svelte opens with worktree selector and prompt input
- [ ] Session launched via claude CLI with selected worktree and prompt
- [ ] Only one active session allowed at a time (single-session model)
- [ ] Session state tracked: launching, active, paused, completed, failed
- [ ] Launching a session while one is active shows warning/confirmation
- [ ] Session metadata recorded (start time, issue link, worktree)

title: 4.1 Terminal Drawer - xterm.js integration
description:
TerminalDrawer.svelte with xterm.js integration. FitAddon for auto-sizing, WebLinksAddon for clickable links, SearchAddon for output search. Copy/paste support, keyboard shortcut to toggle (`). Resizable drawer height, auto-scroll.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TerminalDrawer.svelte renders xterm.js terminal instance
- [ ] FitAddon auto-sizes terminal to drawer dimensions on resize
- [ ] WebLinksAddon makes URLs in output clickable
- [ ] SearchAddon enables Ctrl+F search within terminal output
- [ ] Copy/paste supported (Ctrl+C/V or right-click context menu)
- [ ] Backtick (`) keyboard shortcut toggles drawer open/closed
- [ ] Drawer height resizable via drag handle
- [ ] Terminal auto-scrolls to bottom on new output

title: GitHub CLI Authentication Flow
description:
Implement gh auth status checking, browser-based OAuth flow, and headless device-code flow for servers. Poll gh auth status, show device code UI, handle auth-failed errors. Circuit breaker for rate limiting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `gh auth status` checked on app startup to detect authentication state
- [ ] Unauthenticated state shows login prompt with two flow options
- [ ] Browser-based OAuth flow opens system browser for GitHub login
- [ ] Headless device-code flow displays code and verification URL for server environments
- [ ] Polls `gh auth status` until authentication succeeds or times out
- [ ] Circuit breaker prevents excessive polling under rate limiting
- [ ] Authenticated state persists across page reloads
- [ ] Auth-failed errors show actionable error message

title: 3.13 Gantt Drag/Resize (moved from Phase 2)
description:
Interactive Gantt bar manipulation. Drag bar to change dates, resize bar to change duration, update via bd update --due. Visual feedback, snap to day/week grid, optimistic update with rollback, undo support (Ctrl+Z).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dragging a Gantt bar changes the task's start/end dates
- [ ] Resizing a bar from either edge changes duration
- [ ] Dates snap to day or week grid based on zoom level
- [ ] Optimistic UI update with rollback on `bd update --due` failure
- [ ] Undo support via Ctrl+Z reverts last drag/resize
- [ ] Visual feedback during drag (highlight, date tooltip)
- [ ] Dependent tasks show constraint indicators when dragged past blockers

title: 3.12 Related Tasks Links
description:
Related section in issue detail showing blocks/blocked_by/relates_to. Quick-add related task, navigate to related task, remove relation via bd dep add/remove.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Issue detail shows "Related" section listing blocks, blocked_by, and relates_to links
- [ ] Each related issue shows ID, title, and status
- [ ] Quick-add button to link a related task (search by ID or title)
- [ ] Click on related issue navigates to its detail view
- [ ] Remove relation button calls `bd dep remove`
- [ ] Empty state when no relations exist

title: 3.11 Batch Issue Creation
description:
BatchCreateModal.svelte with markdown list input. Parse titles from lines, apply common properties (labels, priority), create via bd create loop. Progress indicator during creation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BatchCreateModal accepts markdown list input (one title per line)
- [ ] Common properties (labels, priority, type) applied to all created issues
- [ ] Preview shows parsed issue count before creation
- [ ] Issues created sequentially via `bd create` with progress indicator
- [ ] Error on individual issue does not abort remaining items
- [ ] Summary shown on completion (created count, any failures)
- [ ] Modal closes and issue list refreshes on success

title: 3.10 Enhanced Kanban Drag-and-Drop
description:
Multi-card selection with shift-click, drag multiple cards, drop zone highlighting, keyboard-accessible reorder. Drag selection changes status, visual feedback during drag.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shift-click selects multiple cards across the board
- [ ] Dragging a selected card moves all selected cards together
- [ ] Drop zones highlight during drag to indicate valid targets
- [ ] Dropping cards in a new column updates their status via `bd update`
- [ ] Keyboard-accessible reorder (arrow keys + Enter to confirm)
- [ ] Visual feedback during drag (ghost cards, opacity change)
- [ ] Selection clears after successful drop

title: 3.9 Merge Conflict Alerts
description:
Conflict indicator on PR card, conflict details on hover, notification on conflict detection. Red warning icon, shows conflicting files, links to resolution docs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Red warning icon displayed on PR cards with merge conflicts
- [ ] Hover reveals list of conflicting files
- [ ] Link to conflict resolution documentation provided
- [ ] Notification shown when a previously clean PR develops conflicts
- [ ] Conflict state refreshes with PR data (not stale)

title: 3.8 CI Status Display
description:
CIStatusBadge.svelte showing pass/fail/pending (✓/✗/○). Fetch via gh pr checks, hover for check details, click to view on GitHub. Shows on PR cards and linked issue cards, updates periodically (30s), color coded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CIStatusBadge shows pass (✓ green), fail (✗ red), or pending (○ yellow)
- [ ] Badge appears on PR cards and linked issue cards
- [ ] Hover tooltip shows individual check names and statuses
- [ ] Click navigates to checks page on GitHub
- [ ] Status auto-refreshes every 30 seconds while visible
- [ ] Graceful fallback when no checks configured

title: 3.7 PR Merge - Merge PRs from UI
description:
Merge button on PR card, merge strategy selector (merge/squash/rebase), confirmation dialog, delete branch option. Merge via gh pr merge. Only shown for mergeable PRs, shows conflicts warning.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Merge button visible only on mergeable PRs
- [ ] Merge strategy selector offers merge, squash, and rebase options
- [ ] Confirmation dialog shown before merge execution
- [ ] Delete-branch checkbox available in confirmation dialog
- [ ] Conflicts warning displayed for unmergeable PRs (merge button disabled)
- [ ] Merges via `gh pr merge` with selected strategy
- [ ] PR list refreshes after successful merge

title: 3.6 PR Create - Create PRs from UI
description:
CreatePRModal.svelte with branch selector, title/description fields, auto-link current issue. Create via gh pr create. Pre-fills title from branch/issue, description template support, handles errors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CreatePRModal opens with branch selector and title/description fields
- [ ] Title pre-fills from current branch name or linked issue title
- [ ] Description supports template insertion
- [ ] Auto-links current Beads issue in PR body
- [ ] Creates PR via `gh pr create` on submit
- [ ] Validation errors shown inline (missing title, no commits ahead)
- [ ] Modal closes and PR list refreshes on success

title: 3.5 PR Listing - GitHub PR integration
description:
PRList.svelte and PRCard.svelte components. Fetch via gh pr list --json, filter by state (open/closed/merged), link PR to Beads issues. Shows author, branch, status, checks, reviewStatus, linkedIssues.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PRList.svelte fetches PRs via `gh pr list --json`
- [ ] PRCard shows author, branch, status, checks summary, and review status
- [ ] Filter by state: open, closed, merged
- [ ] PRs linked to Beads issues show issue badge
- [ ] Empty state when no PRs match current filter
- [ ] Error state when `gh` CLI not authenticated

title: 3.4 Dependency Arrows on Kanban
description:
Arrow indicators on blocked cards, hover to see blockers, click to navigate. Arrow styling (dashed for relates_to). Blocked cards show red indicator, blocking cards show outgoing arrow.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Blocked cards display red blocking indicator
- [ ] Hover on blocked card reveals list of blocker issues
- [ ] Click on blocker reference navigates to that issue
- [ ] Blocking cards show outgoing arrow indicator
- [ ] relates_to links use dashed arrow style
- [ ] Arrows do not render when no dependencies exist

title: 3.3 Dependency Graph (Basic) - Force-directed visualization
description:
DependencyGraph.svelte using D3.js d3-force. Node coloring by status, edge styling by type (blocks/blocked_by/relates_to), zoom/pan controls. Fetch via bd dep tree --json. Performance: <500ms for 50 nodes, Web Worker for >100 nodes, progressive loading.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DependencyGraph.svelte renders force-directed layout using D3.js d3-force
- [ ] Nodes colored by issue status (open/in_progress/closed)
- [ ] Edges styled by dependency type (solid for blocks, dashed for relates_to)
- [ ] Zoom and pan controls functional (scroll + drag)
- [ ] Graph renders in <500ms for 50 nodes
- [ ] Web Worker used for layouts exceeding 100 nodes
- [ ] Click on node navigates to issue detail
- [ ] Data fetched from `bd dep tree --json`

title: 3.2 Worktree Status (Ahead/Behind)
description:
Ahead/behind badge on worktree card showing divergence from main. Tooltip with commit count, warning for divergent branches. Fetch via git rev-list. Shows '↑3 ↓2' format, updates on fetch/pull.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ahead/behind badge displays on each WorktreeCard (e.g., "↑3 ↓2")
- [ ] Tooltip shows full commit count details on hover
- [ ] Divergent branches show warning indicator
- [ ] Badge updates after fetch/pull operations
- [ ] Worktrees at same commit as main show no badge (clean state)

title: 3.1 Worktree Listing
description:
WorktreeList.svelte and WorktreeCard.svelte components. Display git worktrees with branch, path, head, isMain, beadsRedirect status. Fetch via bd worktree list --json, status via git status --porcelain. Click to open in terminal/editor.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WorktreeList.svelte renders all git worktrees returned by `bd worktree list --json`
- [ ] WorktreeCard shows branch name, path, HEAD commit, isMain badge, and beadsRedirect status
- [ ] Dirty worktrees display modified file count from `git status --porcelain`
- [ ] Click on worktree card opens terminal or editor at that path
- [ ] Empty state shown when no worktrees exist
- [ ] Loading skeleton displayed while fetching worktree data

title: 2.14 Quick Filters (Presets)
description:
Predefined filter presets: My Issues, Overdue, Due This Week, Stale (no updates in 7d). Save custom presets. One-click filter, visual indicator of active preset, presets persist in local storage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Predefined presets available: My Issues, Overdue, Due This Week, Stale (no updates 7d)
- [ ] One-click filter applies preset immediately
- [ ] Active preset visually indicated (highlighted button/chip)
- [ ] Custom presets can be saved from current filter state
- [ ] Presets persist in local storage across sessions
- [ ] Clear button removes active preset and resets filters

title: 2.13 Due Date Management
description:
Due date picker in edit form, due date display on cards, overdue indicator (red), due soon indicator (amber). Date picker with calendar, relative dates supported (+7d), sort by due date.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Due date picker available in issue edit form with calendar widget
- [ ] Relative date shortcuts supported (e.g., +7d, +2w)
- [ ] Due date displayed on issue cards
- [ ] Overdue issues show red indicator
- [ ] Due-soon issues (within 3 days) show amber indicator
- [ ] Issues sortable by due date
- [ ] Due date updates via `bd update --due`

title: 2.10 Hierarchical Sorting
description:
Sort comparator function for issues. Sort by parsed date prefix, sub-sort by parent/child relationship, sub-sort by priority. Earlier dates first, children follow parents, stable sort order.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sort comparator parses date prefix from issue titles
- [ ] Earlier dates sort before later dates
- [ ] Children sorted immediately after their parent issue
- [ ] Sub-sort by priority within same parent group
- [ ] Sort is stable (equal items preserve original order)
- [ ] Sorting works correctly with mixed dated/undated issues

title: 2.8 Progress Bars - Epic/group completion
description:
ProgressBar.svelte component showing percentage complete (done/total), color gradient (red → green), tooltip with details. Shows on epic rows, updates in real-time, accessible with aria attributes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ProgressBar.svelte renders completion percentage (done/total children)
- [ ] Color gradient transitions from red (0%) through amber to green (100%)
- [ ] Tooltip on hover shows detailed breakdown (e.g., "12/18 tasks closed")
- [ ] Progress bars displayed on epic rows in issue lists
- [ ] Bar updates in real-time as child issues are closed
- [ ] Accessible: aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax attributes set

title: 1.13 Owner/Assignee Filter - Autocomplete filtering
description: Autocomplete dropdown with known users from recent issues, 'Me' shortcut for current user, 'Unassigned' filter option. Selection filters immediately, combines with other filters.

title: 1.12 Basic Keyboard Shortcuts
description: Keyboard shortcut manager with j/k navigation, Enter to open, c/n to create, e to edit, Escape to close, / to focus search, ? for help. Help modal shows all shortcuts, no conflicts with browser shortcuts.

title: 1.10 Epics View - Hierarchical epic display
description: EpicsView.svelte with expandable epic rows, progress indicator (done/total children), filter to show only epics. Progress bar reflects completion, click epic to see details.

title: 1.8 Inline Editing - Edit fields in table/card
description: Editable title (click to edit), priority dropdown, assignee dropdown with search. Edit via bd update, Escape cancels. Conflict detection with Refresh & Retry pattern. Optimistic update with rollback.

title: Storybook setup for component documentation
description: Initialize Storybook with SvelteKit, configure essentials addon, a11y addon. Set up stories directory structure for component documentation.

title: [Phase 4] Agent Orchestration
description: Agent session management and verification workflows. 4-week duration covering: terminal drawer with xterm.js, agent session launch/monitoring, verification queue, approve/reject/retry workflows, Claude planning integration.

title: [Phase 3] Git Integration
description: PR management, worktrees, and dependency visualization. 3-week duration covering: worktree listing, PR management via gh CLI, CI status display, dependency graph, batch operations.

title: [Phase 2] Analytics & Timeline
description: Lean metrics and Gantt visualization. 3-week duration covering: Metrics engine, CFD/Lead Time/Throughput/Aging WIP charts, health status badges, date prefix parsing, Gantt chart.

title: [Phase 0] Development Setup
description:
Foundational project setup for Unified Beads WebUI. Includes project scaffolding, development environment, CI/CD pipeline, code quality standards, and documentation.

## Objectives
1. Initialize project scaffolding
2. Configure development environment
3. Set up CI/CD pipeline
4. Establish code quality standards
5. Document development workflow

## Duration
1 day (8-12 hours)

## Success Criteria
- `bun dev` starts without errors
- `bun run check` passes with 0 errors
- `bun run lint` passes with 0 errors
- Pre-commit hooks trigger on commit
- CI pipeline passes on push
- `bd --version` returns version
- `bd sql "SELECT 1"` returns 1

- Dependencies (dep add, dep list)

- SQL access (sql)
- Git operations (hooks, worktree)

### gt Commands to Document
- Status and health
- Mail system
- Convoy management
- Agent operations
- Rig management

### Acceptance Criteria
- Command reference for all wrapped commands
- Input validation rules
- Output parsing patterns
- Error handling for each command
- Timeout and retry policies

### References
- ./references/gastown-webuis.md
- ./references/borrowable-components.md

title: [Phase 6] Create Domain-Specific Components (Gantt, Graph, Activity)
description:
# Phase 6: Domain-Specific Components

Create components specific to the Looms application domain (project management, Gantt charts, dependency graphs).

## Why This Comes Last
Domain components build on ALL previous phases. They use badges, avatars, progress bars, and follow the established design tokens.

## Components to Create

### Gantt Components

**Gantt/Bar** (variable width, 32px height)
Layers:
- Background (status-colored, radius-sm)
- Label text (white or dark, text-sm)
- Left border accent (4px, priority-colored)

Color mapping:
- Open: info (#3B82F6)
- In Progress: warning (#F59E0B)
- Review: purple (#8B5CF6)
- Done: success (#10B981)
- Blocked: error (#EF4444)

**Gantt/BarWithProgress** (variable width, 32px height)
Layers:
- Track (neutral-200, radius-sm)
- Progress fill (status-colored, radius-sm left)
- Label text
- Progress percentage text

**Gantt/Milestone** (24x24)
Layers:
- Diamond shape (rotated square)
- Status-colored fill

**Gantt/DayColumn** (40px width, variable height)
Layers:
- Background (white or neutral-50 for weekends)
- Optional: Today indicator (error color vertical line)

### Graph Components (Dependency Graph)

**Graph/Node** (120x48)
Layers:
- Background (white, radius-lg, shadow)
- Status indicator bar (top, 4px, status-colored)
- Issue ID text (text-xs, neutral-500)
- Title text (text-sm, truncated)

**Graph/NodeSelected** (120x48)
Layers:
- Same as Graph/Node
- Border: 2px primary-500
- Shadow: larger

**Graph/Edge** (variable)
Layers:
- Line (neutral-300, 2px)
- Arrow head (neutral-300)

**Graph/EdgeHighlighted** (variable)
Layers:
- Line (primary-500, 2px)
- Arrow head (primary-500)

### Activity Components

**Activity/Item** (360x64)
Layers:
- Avatar/Small (left)
- User name text (text-md, font-weight 500)
- Action text (text-md, neutral-500)
- Timestamp text (text-xs, neutral-400, right)

**Activity/Container** (360x200)
Layers:
- Background (white, radius-lg, border)
- Title text ("Recent Activity", text-md, font-weight 600)
- 3x Activity/Item instances
- Padding: space-4

### Empty State

**EmptyState** (280x160) - Verify/Rebuild
Layers:
- Background (neutral-50, radius-xl)
- Icon (large, neutral-300)
- Title text (text-lg, font-weight 600, neutral-500)
- Description text (text-md, neutral-400)

## Checklist
- [ ] Create Gantt/Bar with status coloring
- [ ] Create Gantt/BarWithProgress with track + fill
- [ ] Create Gantt/Milestone diamond shape
- [ ] Create Gantt/DayColumn with weekend variant
- [ ] Create Graph/Node with status indicator
- [ ] Create Graph/NodeSelected with selection border
- [ ] Create Graph/Edge with arrow
- [ ] Create Graph/EdgeHighlighted variant
- [ ] Rebuild Activity/Item with Avatar + text
- [ ] Rebuild Activity/Container with title + items
- [ ] Verify/rebuild EmptyState with icon + text
- [ ] Test status color variations
- [ ] Export all domain components as PNGs

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Gantt/Bar colors match status (6 variants work)
2. Gantt/BarWithProgress shows visual progress
3. Graph/Node shows issue preview with status
4. Graph/Edge arrow points in correct direction
5. Activity/Item shows user action with timestamp
6. All components use design token colors
7. Text is overridable where appropriate

## Dependencies
- Phase 0 (Design Tokens) - all colors, especially status colors
- Phase 1 (Icons) - for empty state icon
- Phase 2 (Atomic) - Avatar/Small, Progress/Bar concepts
- Phase 4 (Composite) - similar card patterns

## Blocked By
- looms-jba ([Phase 0] Design Tokens)
- looms-g47 ([Phase 2] Atomic Components)
- looms-pfn ([Phase 4] Composite Components)

## Blocks
- Wireframe updates (Gantt view, Graph view, Dashboard activity)

title: Charts Library: Pie/Donut Chart Symbol
description:
Create a reusable pie/donut chart symbol for proportional data visualization.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Data I/O, Flow, and Schema
- [ ] Define input schema: `{ segments: { label: string, value: number, color: string }[], innerRadius?: number }`
- [ ] Support 2-8 segments
- [ ] Auto-calculate percentages

### Math: Value → Coordinates (Arc Generation)
- [ ] `startAngle = previousEndAngle` (cumulative)
- [ ] `endAngle = startAngle + (value / total) * 2π`
- [ ] `arcPath = describeArc(cx, cy, outerR, innerR, startAngle, endAngle)`

### Arc Generation
- [ ] SVG arc command: `A rx ry rotation large-arc-flag sweep-flag x y`
- [ ] Inner radius = 0 for pie, > 0 for donut
- [ ] Segment gap: 2px visual separation

### Inventory of Shapes
- [ ] Segment/{label} (Path) - arc segments
- [ ] InnerCircle (Oval) - donut hole (if applicable)
- [ ] CenterValue (Text) - total/primary value
- [ ] CenterLabel (Text) - descriptor
- [ ] Legend/Color{n} (Rectangle) - legend swatches
- [ ] {label} (Text) - legend labels
- [ ] Count/{label} (Text) - legend values

### Variablization API
- [ ] Segment colors overridable
- [ ] Inner radius (pie vs donut)
- [ ] Center content configurable
- [ ] Legend position (right, bottom)

### Color/Palette Tokens
- [ ] Categorical palette: blue, amber, purple, green, gray
- [ ] Status palette option: open, in-progress, review, done

### Typography
- [ ] Center value: 20px bold
- [ ] Center label: 11px regular gray-600
- [ ] Legend label: 11px regular
- [ ] Legend value: 11px semibold

title: Charts Library: Sparkline Symbol
description:
Create a compact inline sparkline symbol for trend visualization in tables/cards.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Data I/O, Flow, and Schema
- [ ] Define input schema: `{ values: number[], color?: string, showEndValue?: boolean }`
- [ ] Support 5-30 data points
- [ ] Auto-scale to min/max values

### Math: Value → Coordinates
- [ ] `pointX = padding + (index / (count - 1)) * (width - 2*padding)`
- [ ] `pointY = padding + (1 - (value - min) / (max - min)) * (height - 2*padding)`

### Path Generation (Lines)
- [ ] Single continuous path
- [ ] No axes or grid (minimal)
- [ ] Optional area fill below line

### Inventory of Shapes
- [ ] SparklineBg (Rectangle) - subtle background
- [ ] Line (Path) - trend line
- [ ] EndDot (Oval) - final value indicator
- [ ] Value (Text) - optional end value

### Variablization API
- [ ] Line color overridable
- [ ] End dot visibility
- [ ] Value display toggle
- [ ] Area fill toggle

### Color/Palette Tokens
- [ ] Default: blue-500
- [ ] Positive trend: green-500
- [ ] Negative trend: red-500

### Typography
- [ ] Value: 11px semibold, right-aligned

title: Charts Library: Sparkline Symbol
description:
Create reusable Sparkline Sketch symbol at Components/Chart/Sparkline for inline mini-charts.

**What**: Compact line chart without axes or labels, designed for inline use within cards and table cells. Shows trend direction at a glance without taking full chart space.
**Visual spec**: Frame 80x24. Single-color line with optional area fill below. No axes, labels, or grid. Optional dot at current (rightmost) value.
**Overrides**: Line color, area fill toggle, trend direction (up/down/flat for placeholder states).
**Used in**: Issue cards (velocity trend), dashboard summary widgets, table cells showing metric trends.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Data I/O, Flow, and Schema
- [ ] Define input schema: `{ values: number[] }`
- [ ] Minimal configuration (compact by design)
- [ ] Accept data via: hardcoded, JSON file, or script injection

### Math: Values → Coordinates
- [ ] Scale to fit within small bounds (e.g., 80x20px)
- [ ] Normalize values to min/max range
- [ ] Optional: show as % change from first value

### Path Generation for Lines
- [ ] Generate single Path connecting all points
- [ ] Support line only or line + area fill
- [ ] Optional: highlight min/max points

### Inventory of Included Shapes
- [ ] Line path
- [ ] Optional: area fill below line
- [ ] Optional: end point marker
- [ ] Optional: reference line (baseline)

### Variablization API/Table for Symbols
| Override | Type | Default | Description |
|----------|------|---------|-------------|
| pointCount | number | 7 | Data points |
| showArea | boolean | false | Fill area under line |
| showEndpoint | boolean | true | Highlight last value |

### Typography Variablization
| Element | Token | Default |
|---------|-------|---------|
| Value label (if shown) | `chart.sparkline.value` | Inter 10px Gray-600 |

### Color/Palette Variablization
- [ ] Line color (positive/negative based on trend)
- [ ] Area fill with opacity
- [ ] Map to design tokens: `chart.sparkline.up`, `chart.sparkline.down`

### Vector Shape Generation
- [ ] Line as single Path shape
- [ ] Exportable as SVG
- [ ] Layer naming: `Chart/Sparkline`

title: Charts Library: Pie/Donut Chart Symbol
description:
Create reusable Pie/Donut Chart Sketch symbol at Components/Chart/PieDonutChart for the Charts library.

**What**: Circular chart for showing proportional distribution. Donut variant includes center label for total count. Used for status distribution, priority breakdown, and label distribution views.
**Visual spec**: Frame 200x200. Supports pie (filled) and donut (with center hole) variants. Up to 6 segments with distinct colors. Center label in donut mode shows total or primary metric. Legend displayed externally (uses Chart/Legend component).
**Overrides**: Pie vs donut toggle, segment count (2-6), segment colors, center label text (donut only).
**Used in**: Dashboard sidebar widgets, status distribution summary, priority breakdown.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Data I/O, Flow, and Schema
- [ ] Define input schema: `{ segments: { label: string, value: number, color?: string }[] }`
- [ ] Calculate percentages from values
- [ ] Support minimum segment size (avoid tiny slices)
- [ ] Accept data via: hardcoded, JSON file, or script injection

### Math: Values → Coordinates
- [ ] Convert values to percentages of total
- [ ] Calculate start/end angles for each segment
- [ ] Convert polar coordinates to cartesian for arc paths
- [ ] Support configurable start angle (default: -90° / top)

### Arc Generation for Pies/Donuts
- [ ] Generate arc paths using SVG arc commands
- [ ] Calculate large-arc-flag based on segment size
- [ ] Support inner radius for donut (0 = pie, >0 = donut)
- [ ] Optional: exploded segments (offset from center)

### Inventory of Included Shapes
- [ ] Container frame
- [ ] Arc/wedge shapes for each segment
- [ ] Center hole (for donut)
- [ ] Percentage labels (inside or outside)
- [ ] Legend with color swatches
- [ ] Optional: center label (total, title)

### Variablization API/Table for Symbols
| Override | Type | Default | Description |
|----------|------|---------|-------------|
| segmentCount | number | 4 | Number of segments |
| innerRadius | number | 0 | 0=pie, 0.6=donut |
| showLabels | boolean | true | Show percentage labels |
| showLegend | boolean | true | Show legend |
| startAngle | number | -90 | Start angle in degrees |

### Variablization API/Table for Components
| Component | Overridable Properties |
|-----------|----------------------|
| Segment | fill color, stroke color |
| Label | text, color, position |
| Legend | position, orientation |
| CenterLabel | text, font size |

### Typography Variablization
| Element | Token | Default |
|---------|-------|---------|
| Percentage labels | `chart.value.label` | Inter 11px Bold White/Gray-900 |
| Legend text | `chart.legend.text` | Inter 11px Gray-700 |
| Center label | `chart.center.label` | Inter 18px Bold Gray-900 |
| Center subtitle | `chart.center.subtitle` | Inter 11px Gray-500 |

### Color/Palette Variablization
- [ ] Segment colors array
- [ ] Map to design tokens: `chart.pie.segment1`, etc.
- [ ] Support categorical color schemes
- [ ] Dark mode variants

### Plugin Logic for Data Source Updates
- [ ] Script to regenerate arcs from data
- [ ] Animate segment transitions
- [ ] Sort segments by size (optional)

### Vector Shape Generation
- [ ] Segments as Path shapes with arc commands
- [ ] Clean arc generation without artifacts
- [ ] Exportable as SVG
- [ ] Layer naming: `Chart/Pie/{segment}`

title: Implement search UI with autocomplete
description:
Create an enhanced search input with autocomplete, syntax highlighting, and suggestions.

- Autocomplete for field values (status:open, status:in_progress, etc.)
- Autocomplete for operators (:, :>=, :<, :re:/, etc.)

- Error tooltips for parse errors
- Search history (recent queries)
- Saved searches

## UI Components
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 🔍 status:open prior|                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ priority:      Field                            │
│ priority:1     P1 - Critical                    │
│ priority:>=    Greater than or equal            │
│ priority:1..3  Range (1 to 3)                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Features
- Dropdown appears on field: prefix
- Shows valid values for current field
- Keyboard navigation (up/down/enter/escape)
- Debounced search execution (300ms)

`src/lib/components/search/SearchAutocomplete.svelte`

Parent: looms-i9d
Blocked by: looms-3ip (query parser)

- [ ] Field value autocomplete shows valid values for current field

- [ ] Syntax highlighting for valid/invalid query parts
- [ ] Error tooltips display parse errors inline

title: Implement hybrid search with cache window
Implement server-side filtering endpoint
Implement client-side filtering with cache
description:
Implement the client-side filtering layer that works on cached issues.

## Requirements
- Filter issues already loaded in browser memory
- Instant response (<50ms) for common queries
- Support all field filters: status, priority, type, assignee
- Support sorting on any field
- Integrate with existing appStore filtering

## Integration
```typescript
// In appStore
const filteredByQuery = $derived.by(() => {
  if (!searchQuery) return filtered;
  const ast = parseQuery(searchQuery);
  return executeClientQuery(ast, filtered);
});
```

## Location
`src/lib/stores/app.svelte.ts` (integration)
`src/lib/search/client-executor.ts` (executor)

Parent: looms-i9d
Blocked by: looms-9vm (client executor)

title: Fix npm publish prepare script workaround
description:
The release workflow uses `--ignore-scripts` when publishing to npm to work around husky not being installed in CI.

## Problem
The `prepare` script in package.json runs `husky`, which fails in CI because husky is a devDependency not installed during `npm publish`.

## Current Workaround
Using `npm publish --ignore-scripts` skips all lifecycle scripts.

## Proper Solutions to Evaluate
1. **Conditional prepare script**: Use `is-ci` package to skip husky in CI
   ```json
   "prepare": "is-ci || husky"
   ```

2. **Use pinst**: Package specifically designed for this
   ```json
   "prepublishOnly": "pinst --disable",
   "postpublish": "pinst --enable"
   ```

3. **Check for .git directory**:
   ```json
   "prepare": "node -e \"if(require('fs').existsSync('.git'))require('child_process').execSync('husky')\""
   ```

4. **Separate postinstall from prepare**: Move husky to postinstall which doesn't run during publish

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Remove `--ignore-scripts` from release workflow
- [ ] Husky still works for local development
- [ ] npm publish works in CI without errors
- [ ] Solution is documented in ADR or CLAUDE.md

title: Fix async test timing issues in Svelte 5 component tests
description:

Investigation needed: 1) Understanding Svelte 5 runes and  timing in jsdom, 2) Proper use of tick() and flushSync() for test synchronization, 3) Potential testing-library/svelte version compatibility issues. Affected tests: FilterPanel integration, TextSearch debounce, row selection, search clear.

title: Implement LSP-style syntax hints for search input
description:
Add LSP-inspired syntax hints, diagnostics, and hover information to the search input.

## Requirements
- Real-time syntax validation as user types
- Inline error indicators (red underline for invalid syntax)
- Hover tooltips explaining operators and fields
- Quick fixes for common errors
- Field documentation on hover

## Diagnostics
```typescript
interface SearchDiagnostic {
  range: { start: number; end: number };
  severity: 'error' | 'warning' | 'hint';
  message: string;
  quickFixes?: QuickFix[];
}

// Example diagnostics:
// 'statsu:open' → Error: Unknown field 'statsu'. Did you mean 'status'?
// 'priority:high' → Warning: 'high' is not a valid priority. Use 1-4.
// 'created:yesterday' → Hint: Use relative dates like '<1d' or '<7d'
```

## Hover Information
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 🔍 status:open priority:>=2                     │
│        ↑                                        │
│ ┌────────────────────────────────┐              │
│ │ Field: status                  │              │
│ │ Type: string                   │              │
│ │ Values: open, in_progress,     │              │
│ │         review, done, closed   │              │
│ │                                │              │
│ │ Examples:                      │              │
│ │   status:open                  │              │
│ │   status:open|in_progress      │              │
│ │   \!status:closed               │              │
│ └────────────────────────────────┘              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Quick Fixes
- Typo correction: 'statsu' → 'status'
- Value suggestion: 'priority:high' → 'priority:1'
- Syntax help: 'status = open' → 'status:open'

## Location
`src/lib/search/diagnostics.ts`
`src/lib/components/search/SearchHints.svelte`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Real-time syntax validation highlights errors as user types
- [ ] Invalid syntax shows red underline with error range
- [ ] Hover tooltip explains operators and available fields
- [ ] Quick fixes offered for common errors (typos, wrong values)
- [ ] Field documentation shown on hover (type, valid values, examples)
- [ ] Diagnostics module at src/lib/search/diagnostics.ts
- [ ] SearchHints component at src/lib/components/search/SearchHints.svelte
